updated_conclusion: The worker-name helper at 0x14066DC10 is now split into two clearer responsibilities: 0x14066E030 initializes or refreshes the candidate worker payload/container state, while 0x14066DDF0 performs sorted 24-byte record lookup-or-insert under the parent object's +0x70 vector and returns the value-pair slot that 0x14066DC10 replaces. The wider 0x14001C520 routine is the full client-object constructor/initializer; it names Js5WorkerThread, MixerWorker, and VorbisWorker in three repeated registration/provider blocks but does not itself call 0x14066DC10.

tooling_milestone.change: Added native ordered call-instruction extraction for PE function ranges. It reports direct CALL rel32 targets with executable-section filtering and .pdata target attribution, plus common indirect forms such as RIP-relative import calls, register calls, and vtable-style memory calls.
tooling_milestone.why_it_matters: The worker/bootstrap milestone needs ordered per-function call chains, not just inverse target fan-in. This command turns Ghidra window call lists into repeatable Reverser artifacts.

evidence_source: GhidraCallsiteArgsPy scratch output for 0x14066DCEA and the Js5WorkerThread local builder callsites

ordered_calls.role: Prepares or validates name material using [candidate_record+8]+8 and the worker-name byte length context.
Initializes/refreshed candidate worker payload state before publication; receives parent object in RDX and the stack copy of the candidate pair in R8.
Looks up or creates the parent +0x70 keyed slot using the dword at [candidate_payload+0x20].
Installs/replaces the ref-counted pair in the returned slot, releasing the previous object through vtable +0x8/+0x10 when present.

worker_name_helper_call_chain.bounded_inference: 0x14066DC10 is best modeled as a named worker-record publisher. It validates/activates the payload, finds a stable id-keyed slot under the parent object, then publishes a ref-counted pair into that slot.

observed_behavior: Stores the parent object argument into [RCX+0xD0] and clears [RCX+0xDB].
Branches on [RCX+0xFC] == 1, with both branches allocating/copying the caller-provided R8 tuple and building a ref-counted wrapper using vtable/data pointer 0x140B9FCC8.
Calls 0x1407CD29C with callback/code pointer 0x14066F3F0, then installs the resulting wrapper pair into RCX+0x28 through 0x1400A8090.
Releases temporary ref-counted wrappers through vtable slots +0x8 and +0x10.
On success copies dword [RCX+0x30]+8 into [RCX+0x20] and returns AL=1; failure paths call assertion/throw helpers and INT3.
candidate_payload_initializer_0x14066e03.confidence: medium-high for field writes, call sequence, and ref-counted wrapper behavior; medium for the exact semantic name of the RCX object

record_layout.container: RCX points at a begin/end/capacity vector-like header.

observed_behavior: Uses magic multiplier 0x2AAAAAAAAAAAAAAB and shifts to divide byte spans by 0x18.
Binary-searches records by the dword key read from RDX.
Returns the existing record+0x8 value-pair slot when the key is already present.
Creates a zeroed record for a missing key either by appending in place, inserting through 0x14066F140, or growing/rebalancing through 0x14066E560.
Releases any temporary ref-counted pair stored in the stack record and returns RDI+0x8.
keyed_slot_lookup_0x14066ddf0.confidence: high for record size, key offset, vector header shape, and return slot; medium for exact container ownership

client_bootstrap_constructor_0x14001c520.object_size_evidence: The sibling destructor/deallocator at 0x14001CDE0 passes 0x53818 as the delete size.
observed_behavior: Initializes a large client object rooted in RDI, including offsets around +0x18D08, +0x19A20, and +0x53580 through +0x53810.
Publishes a global pointer at 0x140E57C90 to a client subobject around +0x18D08 before worker setup.
Builds three repeated worker registration/provider blocks for Js5WorkerThread, MixerWorker, and VorbisWorker.

Each worker block calls 0x14066D010 to create a worker/provider object, 0x140032150 to build a local small-string copy of the worker name, 0x1400D24B0 to attach the local name to the worker object, and 0x1400D1990 to attach the worker-specific descriptor/provider pointer.

Each worker block optionally dispatches through global 0x140E57B40 via vtable slot +0x20 with the local name and a count/length derived from the same global object, then releases the temporary descriptor pointer.
client_bootstrap_constructor_0x14001c520.relationship_to_0x14066dc10: This constructor names and attaches the same three worker families that later reach 0x14066DC10 through their setup/prelude routines. It is a bootstrap-side registry/provider construction path, not the name-publisher helper itself.

observed_behavior: Checks an incoming string-like argument against --compileshader.
Allocates either a smaller 0x18D20-shaped client variant or the full 0x53818 client object.
Calls 0x14001C520 only on the full allocation path.
Publishes the selected client object through globals 0x140E57B58 and 0x140C572F8, then performs follow-up virtual initialization through slots +0x18 and sometimes +0x48.
bootstrap_allocator_0x14001acc0.confidence: medium-high for allocation sizes, globals, and constructor call; medium for --compileshader branch semantics

relationship_to_previous_milestones.worker-name-registration-helper: Splits the previously named helper into the activation step and the sorted parent-slot insertion step.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.js5-worker-provider-cluster-lifecycle: Connects Js5WorkerThread provider labeling to the full client constructor that also names MixerWorker and VorbisWorker.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.pe-direct-call-caller-attribution: The new pe-function-calls command complements inverse fan-in by preserving forward call order inside each function range.

next_targets: Trace 0x14066E560 and 0x14066F140 to fully name the grow/insert helpers used by the 0x18-byte sorted record vector.
Trace 0x14066F3F0 and 0x1407CD29C to identify the callback/task wrapper built by 0x14066E030.
Trace 0x14066D010, 0x1400D24B0, and 0x1400D1990 to connect the 0x14001C520 worker-label blocks to concrete worker/provider object lifetimes.
Use pe-function-calls on the Js5WorkerThread, MixerWorker, and VorbisWorker setup functions to align constructor-side labels with the name-publisher helper callsites.
